text.title: Structured Output Patterns — Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure from input brief to signed-off output code.

list.item: Input: Codebase.
Action: List every structured-output call site; group by schema.
Output: inventory.csv with (call_site, schema, provider, frequency).
Decision gate: If >50 call sites, prioritise top 10 by traffic.
Input: Provider clients, schemas.
Action: Author class with `.call(schema_cls, prompt, ...) -> schema_cls`; wire metrics + retry + failover.
Output: service.py with tests.
Decision gate: If failover not yet needed, keep single-provider; design the interface allowing future failover.
Input: inventory.csv.
Action: Replace direct calls with service.call(); land migration PRs per schema group.
Output: All call sites routed through service.
Decision gate: If a call site has unique behaviour, expose it via a service kwarg before forking the service.
Input: Streaming requirements.
Action: Author `.stream(schema_cls, prompt, ...) -> Iterator[Partial[schema_cls]]`; use partial-json-parser.
Output: stream() method tested with 3+ schemas.
Decision gate: If no streaming consumer exists, defer this step.
Input: Metrics dashboard.
Action: Track parse_success_rate per schema; alert on regression; iterate on prompts + schemas.
Output: Per-schema SLO dashboard.
Decision gate: If success_rate drops > 5pp, freeze new endpoints + investigate.
